task: Φ* v3 canonical (auto-conditioning HID=8) 3-way SSM cross-substrate measurement: pure-SSM (Mamba) vs hybrid-SSM-attn (Jamba) vs attention-free-recurrent (RWKV-7)
metric_design: AUTO-CONDITIONING canonical: HID_TRUNC = max(2, N//2) = 8 (N=16), eff_dof_full=8, eff_dof_half=0+ridge stabilized

ai21labs/Jamba-v0.1.arch: hybrid SSM + attention (Mamba blocks interleaved with Transformer attention layers)
ai21labs/Jamba-v0.1.note: successful run was jamba_run2 (initial jamba run failed: Fast Mamba kernels missing)

fla-hub/rwkv7-1.5B-world.arch: attention-free recurrent (RWKV-7 Goose, state-space family with linear-time non-attention recurrence)
fla-hub/rwkv7-1.5B-world.deps_workaround: torchvision uninstall + transformers>=4.48.0,<4.55 + flash-linear-attention from git

HR-A_recurrent_iit_positive.reasoning: RWKV-7 phi_star_min = -9.07 (NEG, large magnitude). If recurrent/SSM substrate were uniformly IIT-positive (as Mamba +0.33 and Jamba +3.31 suggested), RWKV-7 should also show POS. It does not. Recurrent-substrate-uniformity hypothesis FAILS within the SSM family.

HR-B_transformer_band_negative.reasoning: RWKV-7 -9.07 falls in the same large-magnitude-NEG band as mistral (-16.7) and gemma (-0.79), distinct from Mamba (+0.33) and Jamba (+3.31). The large-magnitude anti-integration signal is NOT transformer-specific (mistral/gemma) — it appears in attention-free recurrent (RWKV-7) too.

HR-C_border_noise.reasoning: RWKV-7 8/8 partitions are NEG with magnitudes |phi| in [2.28, 9.07], far outside the |phi| < 1 border noise band. This is a stable strong-signal measurement, not noise.

primary_finding: 3-way SSM split: Mamba (+0.33 border POS) and Jamba (+3.31 substantial POS) cluster with qwen3/llama transformer-POS family, while RWKV-7 (-9.07 substantial NEG, all 8/8 partitions NEG) clusters with mistral/gemma transformer-NEG family. The anti-integration signal is NOT architecture-class-specific (transformer vs SSM vs attention-free-recurrent). Within the SSM family alone, RWKV-7 is sign-discordant from Mamba and Jamba.

axes_implication.H6A_transformer_specific_anti_integratio: FURTHER_FALSIFIED — RWKV-7 attention-free recurrent also shows large-NEG. Anti-integration is NOT transformer-attention-specific.
axes_implication.H6B_LLM_substrate_inherent: PARTIALLY_RECONSIDERED — substrate-inherent claim is more nuanced: NEG appears across transformer (mistral/gemma) AND attention-free recurrent (RWKV-7), suggesting some substrate-shared mechanism, but Mamba/Jamba (also recurrent) do NOT show NEG, so simple architecture-class predictor fails.
axes_implication.H8B_independent_speed_vs_phi: STRENGTHENED — RWKV-7's NEG sign vs Mamba/Jamba POS within SSM family further decouples sign from architecture-class. Phi sign appears determined by something orthogonal to recurrent-vs-attention substrate (possibly: training corpus, tokenizer family, scale, layer norm conventions, or RWKV-specific time-mix gating).

next_axes_candidates: Within-RWKV scale sweep: RWKV-7 0.4B vs 1.5B vs 2.9B at same metric — does NEG persist or shift?
Tokenizer ablation: RWKV-7 with re-tokenized GPT-NeoX-style probes vs World tokenizer — isolate tokenizer-vs-architecture causal contribution
RWKV-6 vs RWKV-7 comparison: did the time-mix v6→v7 redesign cause the sign flip relative to Mamba?
Mistral as POS by tokenizer: re-run mistral with RWKV World tokenizer — if mistral flips POS, tokenizer family is a primary determinant
